serverInfo.name: tweetfeed-mcp
serverInfo.description: MCP server exposing the public TweetFeed IOC feed (URLs, domains, IPs, MD5/SHA-256 hashes shared by the infosec community on Twitter/X) as agent tools.

tools.name: query_iocs
tools.description: Query the TweetFeed API for Indicators of Compromise (IOCs: URLs, domains, IPs, MD5/SHA256 hashes) shared by the infosec community on Twitter/X. Returns matching rows with date, researcher handle, type, value, tags, and tweet URL. Required: time (today/week/month). Optional: user, tag, type, limit.

tools.name: check_url
tools.description: Check whether a URL (or substring) appears in the TweetFeed corpus over the past 30 days. Useful for confirming if an observed URL has been flagged by the public infosec Twitter/X community. Case-insensitive substring match.

tools.name: check_ip
tools.description: Check whether an IP address appears in the TweetFeed corpus over the past 30 days. Useful for confirming if an observed IP has been flagged as attacker infrastructure (C2, scanner, phishing host). Substring match against the value field of type=ip IOCs.

tools.name: check_hash
tools.description: Check whether a file hash (MD5 or SHA-256) appears in the TweetFeed corpus over the past 30 days. Hash type auto-detected from length (32 hex = MD5, 64 hex = SHA-256). Exact match on hex value, case-insensitive.

tools.name: list_recent_iocs
tools.description: List TweetFeed IOCs added since a given date, useful for delta-syncing a blocklist or Threat Intelligence pipeline. Required: since (YYYY-MM-DD). Optional: limit, type, tag. Source is the 30-day month window.

tools.name: get_tag_info
tools.description: Bundle of TweetFeed activity for a single tag: aggregate counts across today/week/month/year windows plus the most recent IOCs. Saves the agent from making three separate calls to assemble a tag overview. Required: tag. Optional: limit.

tools.name: get_trending
tools.description: Top tags and IOC-type distribution for a given window (today/week/month/year), computed from the live counts.json aggregate. Useful for 'what is the community talking about right now' queries. Required: window. Optional: limit.

tools.name: enrich_ioc
tools.description: Look up an IOC value: exact lookup over the past 365 days (aggregated: first_seen, last_seen, count, reporters, tags), including AI-generated context (summary, malware family, threat type) when available, with a 30-day substring fallback, auto-detected type (URL / domain / IP / MD5 / SHA-256). Required: value.

tools.name: get_campaigns
tools.description: List AI-clustered threat campaign groupings of the last 7 days of community IOCs. Each campaign groups related IOCs by shared infrastructure, tags, or targeted brand, with an AI-generated name and context. Optional: brand (substring match on targeted_brand), min_confidence (low/medium/high), limit (1-50, default 20). Returns trimmed campaigns with up to 5 sample IOCs each.

tools.name: get_trends
tools.description: IOC trend analytics from the last 31 days: daily volume by type, top moving tags week-over-week (current 7d vs previous 7d), most-abused TLDs among domain IOCs, and new vs recurring indicator ratio. Optional: section (daily/movers/tlds/novelty/all, default all).

dataEndpoints.name: campaigns
dataEndpoints.description: AI-clustered campaign groupings of the last 7 days of community IOCs. Deterministic pre-grouping (shared registered domain, URL path patterns, tags) plus AI naming/context; regenerated daily. `stale: true` means the daily refresh failed and this is the previous day's document.

dataEndpoints.name: stix_2_1_bundles
dataEndpoints.description: Static STIX 2.1 indicator bundles for SIEM/Threat Intelligence platforms. Manifest indexes today/week/month bundles, each a spec-compliant Bundle of Indicator objects with TLP:CLEAR marking. Regenerated every 15 minutes.

dataEndpoints.name: diff_endpoint
dataEndpoints.description: Returns IOCs added after the given ISO 8601 timestamp. Same /{filter1}/{filter2} syntax as /v1/{window}. 410 if since > 365d ago, 400 on malformed ISO.

dataEndpoints.name: per_tag_rss
dataEndpoints.description: One RSS 2.0 feed per active tag (>=1 hit in the last 7 days). Subscribe to a single threat type without polling the firehose.
